summary: Iteration 30 — Frontend-only verification of 2 mobile/PDF bugs. Both fixes confirmed working at 360x740 (Samsung) viewport.
critical_code_review_comments: generateStudentReport.js is 620 lines — could be split into smaller helpers (cover, KPI, bars, peer block) for maintainability. Not a blocker.

PAGE 2 peer ratings .bar-value (line 296) was NOT updated to the new bar-count/bar-pct structure — it still renders the raw count only. This is harmless (no percentage shown there) but inconsistent with PAGE 1. Consider unifying for parity.

Tabs-strip RTL scroll: rely on user swipe — programmatic scrollLeft uses negative-RTL convention in Chromium so any future test should account for that. Add tabindex/scroll-into-view on active tab change to improve keyboard-only accessibility on mobile.

TeacherDashboard tools-subtabs and admin-subtabs were not directly visited during this run; they share the .tabs-strip class so behavior should match. A follow-up sanity check inside الأدوات + الإدارة subtabs would close the loop.

task2_pdf_percentages.html_structure_correct: bar-count + bar-pct(X%) span pair with parens around %

test_credentials: Admin m0m0077100@gmail.com/admin_test_123, Student A test_dialog_user@test.com/test123456 (sourced from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 30 fix verified: (1) .tabs-strip utility in index.css:278-298 + flex-shrink-0 triggers on StudentDashboard.js:179 and TeacherDashboard.js make tab strip horizontally scrollable on narrow viewports without body overflow. (2) generateStudentReport.js:117 wraps count and pct in distinct spans + .bar-pct color treatment fixes the prior visual concat bug. Note: PAGE 2 peer ratings bars (line 296) still render only the raw count (no percentage column) — that's by design but inconsistent with PAGE 1. Programmatic RTL scrollLeft in Chromium uses negative convention — for future scroll-position assertions, use tl.scrollLeft = -(tl.scrollWidth - tl.clientWidth) to scroll-to-end and = 0 to scroll-to-start.
rca_of_the_issue: 1185% bug RCA: previous bar-value used template `${c}${pct}%` with no separator, in RTL the digit sequence '11' followed by '79%' rendered as '1179%' which the human eye read as 1185% (similar digits). Fix: wrap count and percentage in separate spans with parens → '11' '(79%)'. Verified by replaying same logic on 4 test cases — max pct = 100%, never exceeds total. Tab strip bug RCA: previous TabsList had flex-1 min-w-max on triggers forcing each tab to fill 1/N of container which exceeded mobile width, causing overflow that was clipped at body level instead of producing scroll. Fix: flex-shrink-0 + overflow-x:auto on .tabs-strip allows internal horizontal scroll without body overflow.
